depression.description: I often feel like I'm not worth anyone's time, and that leads me to avoid socializing altogether. It's like I convince myself that my presence is unwanted.
When I don't get invited to gatherings, it hits me hard. I can't help but think that my friends don't want me around, and it makes me feel incredibly lonely.
Whenever I feel disappointed by a friend's actions, like Edson reaching out only when it suits him, I just withdraw. It's as if I can't face the possibility of being hurt again.
I find myself seeking reassurance from friends to feel valued, but at the same time, I pull away from them. It's confusing because I want connection, but fear it too.

Delusion.description: I truly believe that I am unlovable and that no one would want to be with me. This thought is so ingrained that it colors every interaction I have.

Delusion.topic: Conditional worth based on social presence
Delusion.description: I feel like my worth is only valid when I'm socially available and upbeat. If I'm not, I assume my friends will abandon me or think less of me.

Delusion.topic: Fear of being forgotten by friends
Delusion.description: There's this constant fear that if I don't reach out, my friends will forget about me. I feel like I have to prove my value to them all the time.
